concepts.name: Array
concepts.short_definition: A contiguous block of memory storing elements of the same data type.

concepts.name: Stack
concepts.short_definition: A linear data structure following the Last-In, First-Out (LIFO) principle.

concepts.name: Push Operation
concepts.short_definition: Adding an element to the top of the stack.

concepts.name: Pop Operation
concepts.short_definition: Removing and returning the element from the top of the stack.

concepts.name: Peek Operation
concepts.short_definition: Returning the element at the top of the stack without removing it.

concepts.name: IsEmpty Check
concepts.short_definition: Determining if the stack contains any elements.

concepts.name: IsFull Check
concepts.short_definition: Determining if the stack has reached its maximum capacity.

concepts.name: Stack Pointer
concepts.short_definition: An index that points to the top element of the stack.

concepts.name: Fixed Size Stack
concepts.short_definition: A stack implemented with an array of a predetermined, fixed size.

concepts.name: Dynamic Size Stack
concepts.short_definition: A stack that can grow or shrink in size as needed (though array implementation typically implies fixed size unless re-allocation is considered).
